etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "in-" + "tolerant" from Latin "in-" + "tolerare" (not bearing, enduring)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Not bearing enduring; experiencing unwilling acceptance and intolerant unwillingness supported by tolerance awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Not bearing → unwilling acceptance → contemporary intolerant unwillingness and intolerant acceptance that benefits from acceptance growth
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Not bearing → unwilling acceptance → intolerant unwillingness → acceptance understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having intolerant unwillingness and intolerant acceptance; experiencing unwilling acceptance and intolerant intolerance calling for tolerance acceptance
contemporary_usage.register: Serious negative; indicates unwillingness calling for tolerance support and acceptance development
